item.title: APTO — Assessing and Predicting Technology Outcomes
item.description:
NSF-funded APTO project (NSF 2404109) on assessing and predicting technology outcomes. We are building data-driven measures and predictive models for technological evolution and translation of knowledge into products.

Role: Researcher
Period: Sep 2025 – Present
Sponsor: National Science Foundation (NSF 2404109)

item.title: URoL:EN — Toward a Unified Theory of Regulatory Functions and Networks
item.description:
NSF-funded URoL:EN project (EF-2133863), developing a unified theory of regulatory functions and networks across biological and social systems. As part of this project, we built a century-scale dataset of the U.S. legal code and analyzed the evolution of its complexity (Jeong et al., Scientific Data, 2026).

Role: Researcher
Period: Sep 2024 – Aug 2025
Sponsor: National Science Foundation (EF-2133863)

item.title: Co-evolutionary Pattern of Production and Technology for Malaysia
item.description:
A World Bank Group project examining the co-evolutionary pattern of production and technology for Malaysia, with a focus on identifying opportunities for phase and green transition.

Role: Researcher
Sponsor: World Bank Group

item.title: Patterns of Economic Diversification via Coevolution of Production and Technology
item.description:
A World Bank Group project mapping patterns of economic diversification through the coevolution of production and technology. The work led to the comparative study of Korea and Viet Nam (Aridi et al., World Bank Report, 2024).

Role: Researcher
Sponsor: World Bank Group
